Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product in the sales dashboard.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer in the sales dashboard.

Sale.title: Sale
Sale.description: Represents a sale transaction in the sales dashboard.

id.description: Unique identifier for the sale transaction.

amount.description: Total amount of the sale transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the invoice, often formatted (e.g., INV/2024/001).

soNumber.description: Sales Order number related to this invoice.

poNumber.description: Purchase Order number from the customer.

customer.description: Name of the customer being invoiced.

date.description: The date the invoice was issued.

amount.description: The total amount of the invoice.

status.description: The current status of the invoice.

spdNumber.description: The SPD (Surat Pengantar Dokumen) number associated with this invoice, if any.

ownerId.description: The UID of the user who owns this document.

definition.description: Stores product information. Write access is restricted to users with admin privileges (checked via /users/{uid}).

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

definition.description: Stores customer data. Only the user who created it (ownerId) can read/write their own data.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

definition.description: Stores sale transaction data. Only the user who created it (ownerId) can read/write the sale data.

params.name: saleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sale transaction.

definition.description: Stores invoice documents. Only the user who created it (ownerId) can access it.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the invoice.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, including their role.

params.name: uid
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to securely manage product, customer, and sales data for the sales dashboard application. It prioritizes Authorization Independence, clarity, and ease of querying.

**Authorization Independence:**

*   Sales are stored directly under a `sales` collection. To enforce security without relying on hierarchical `get()` calls, `customerId` is denormalized inside the Sale document. This allows rules to validate that the `customerId` matches the `request.auth.uid` when creating or modifying sales, without needing to read the customer document.

**Structural Segregation:**

*   Each entity type (products, customers, sales) is stored in its own top-level collection. This segregation simplifies security rules because each collection has a distinct access pattern.

**Access Modeling:**

*   **Customers:** Customers are stored in the `/customers/{customerId}` collection. This path-based ownership allows simple rules enforcing that only the authenticated user (with `uid` equal to `customerId`) can read/write their customer data.
*   **Sales:** Sales are stored in the `/sales/{saleId}` collection. Security is enforced using denormalized `customerId` to restrict access to sales associated with the authenticated user.
*   **Products:** Products are stored in the `/products/{productId}` collection. Assuming all products are publicly readable but only admins can create/update them, a separate `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection controls administrative privileges.  Rules on the `/products` collection check for the existence of a document in `/roles_admin/{request.auth.uid}` to grant write access.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   The structure supports secure listing of sales associated with a specific customer because the `customerId` is available in each `Sale` document. This allows rules to filter sales based on the `request.auth.uid` (which represents the `customerId`). Products are secured based on admin roles.

**Invariants:**

*   Timestamps could be added to documents (e.g., `createdAt`, `updatedAt`) to track changes. Security rules can enforce that `createdAt` is only set on creation and `updatedAt` is always updated on modifications.
